name: Pumble
one_line: Team messaging and collaboration platform (Slack alternative) by CAKE.com with an app/API and event system.
self_serve_detail: Create an app/addon from scratch in Pumble workspace settings (free plan supported), choose user/bot scopes, install, then copy the App key plus user/bot tokens. OAuth2 authorization-code flow; no human approval required.
api_surface: REST broad (api-ga.pumble.com v3) plus WebSocket events

name: Discord
one_line: Chat/voice/gaming community platform exposing a REST HTTP API plus a WebSocket Gateway for bots and OAuth2 apps.
self_serve_detail: Create an app free in the Discord Developer Portal; a bot token is issued instantly and OAuth2 client_id/secret are free. Privileged intents (e.g., message content) require a toggle/approval, but basic API use needs no human review.

name: Telegram
one_line: Cloud messaging platform whose Bot API lets developers build bots via @BotFather.
self_serve_detail: Message @BotFather with /newbot and immediately receive a free bot token; no approval or review. The token authenticates every Bot API call via https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/<method>.
api_surface: HTTPS Bot API (simple REST-style, broad method coverage)

name: Google Ads
one_line: Google's advertising API for programmatically managing campaigns, ads, and reporting.
self_serve_detail: Sign up for a developer token in the Google Ads API Center (free); Test Account Access is auto-granted with no review. Calling production accounts requires applying for Basic/Standard Access, which needs approval/review (typically 5-10 business days; Explorer Access sometimes auto-granted). The API itself is free to use.
blocker: Production access requires App Review approval of the developer token (Basic/Standard Access); only Test Account Access is auto-granted.

name: Meta Ads / Marketing API
one_line: Meta's Marketing API for managing Facebook/Instagram ads, audiences, and reporting.
self_serve_detail: Add the Marketing API product to a free Meta app; Limited access is auto-granted and standard access to ads_read/ads_management for your own ad account is auto-approved. Advanced access (managing other businesses' accounts) and Tech Provider status require App Review plus Business Verification. The official MCP (mcp.facebook.com/ads) gives OAuth access to your own account with no App Review.
blocker: Advanced access (managing other businesses' ad accounts) and Tech Provider status require App Review + Business Verification; full third-party use is gated, though own-account access via the official MCP needs no review.
